Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies Inc.'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint [Re-Set From 3/26/2020 Per Agreement Of Parties; Email Dated 1/15/2021] [Motion Is Fully Briefed.]
Off Calendar Jan-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DUNCAN MACDONALD, Esq of MacDonald Law (Tel: 415-956-7777) for Defendant Hayden AI Technologies, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies Inc.'s Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Hayden AI Technologies, Inc.'s demurrers to causes of action 1-8 and 10-11 are overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that defendant is the successor of plaintiff's employer, Caruma Technologies Inc., and defendant is liable for Caruma's debts.

The law is clear that "[i]f a corporation organizes another corporation with practically the same shareholders and directors, transfers all the assets but does not pay all the first corporation's debts, and continues to carry on the same business, the separate entities may be disregarded and the new corporation held liable for the obligations of the old. [Citations.]" (McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Assoc., Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 754.) Plaintiff alleges that Carson dominated both entities and the two corporations had the same CEO (Carson), same office, and same business. (Third Amended Complaint, pars. 12, 30-34.) Reading the complaint liberally per Code of Civil Procedure Section 452, plaintiff sufficiently pleads successor liability. Defendant cites no authority holding that since defendant did not exist during plaintiff's tenure with Caruma and was created approximately a year after plaintiff resigned, there can be no successor liability as a matter of law. Successor liability may still exist despite the temporal issue if the McClellan factors exist. Because plaintiff successfully pleads the successor liability claims, the court need not address the alter ego issue at this point.

The court rejects defendant's argument that plaintiff is an exempt employee and cannot sue under the adduced Labor Code provisions. Defendant raises a factual issue regarding the facts and circumstances of plaintiff's job and his title is not dispositive. (See Ramirez v. Yosemite Water Co., Inc. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 785, 794 [whether exemption applies is an affirmative defense that is a mixed question of law and fact].)

The court overrules the demurrer for uncertainty. Such demurrers are disfavored and defendant can reasonably respond to the amended complaint. Given the allegations regarding Carson and the entities, the key facts are presumptively within defendant's knowledge.

The demurrer to the 9th cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (See Code of Civil Procedure Section 430.10(g).) The type of contract cannot be ascertained and plaintiff has leave to plead whether it was oral, written, or implied.

The Court will review the prevailing party's proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies' [Ex Parte] Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against It [Per 9/19 Ost]
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies' [Ex Parte] Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against It [Per 9/19 Ost]

Appearances:
Drew Lewis, Esq. 650-665-9000 x2 of Lewis Law for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Duncan MacDonald, Esq. 415-956-7777 of MacDonald for Defendant Hayden Ai Technologies.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues re: Court's comments on Counsel's conduct.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Hayden AI Technologies, Inc.'s motion to vacate the default entered against it on September 13, 2019 is granted, and the September 30, 2019 trial date is vacated.

This action was filed on February 28, 2018. Hayden was first named in the second amended complaint filed on July 11, 2019, and was served on August 7, 2019. On September 6, 2019, Hayden retained counsel to represent it, who immediately contacted plaintiff's counsel and requested a 30-day extension of time to respond to the complaint on its behalf, and also suggested that counsel discuss continuing the September 30, 2019 trial date. Plaintiff's counsel would agree only to a one-day continuance for Hayden to respond to the complaint, and refused to discuss continuing the trial date. Because Hayden was not served until shortly before the trial date, plaintiff's counsel's refusal to grant it a reasonable extension of time to respond to the complaint effectively precluded the parties from meeting and conferring regarding the grounds for a possible demurrer to the complaint. Further, Hayden acted diligently to seek a continuance of the trial date and an extension of time to respond to the second amended complaint. Under the circumstances, the default must be set aside. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.41(a)(2), 473(b).)

The Court expressly disapproves plaintiff's counsel's unprofessional and uncivil conduct in, among other things, refusing to grant opposing counsel a reasonable extension of time to respond to the second amended complaint, refusing to meet and confer regarding the grounds for a possible demurrer, and refusing to agree to a continuance of the trial date, despite having first named and served Hayden just weeks before that date. (See Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.4th 127.) The Court expects better of counsel.

Hayden shall have 30 days following entry of this order to respond to the second amended complaint. If it elects to file a demurrer, the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person or by telephone for the purpose of determining whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the questions to be raised in the demurrer. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.41(a).)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
